description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crustacean, likely a species of shrimp or lobster, featuring a segmented body with a pronounced cephalothorax and elongated abdomen. The exoskeleton is primarily a pale beige to light brown color, with subtle variations and some darker mottling along the back. Two prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, one noticeably larger than the other, and several walking legs are visible along the sides. The head region displays two long, thin antennae and small, dark eyes set on short stalks. The underside reveals fine appendages and a slightly translucent quality to the shell. The overall form is elongated and slightly curved, with a hard, textured surface typical of marine crustaceans.
description: A pale, segmented crustacean with large claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured exoskeleton. The body is elongated and features multiple walking legs and subtle color variations.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the curvature of the abdomen.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the face, both claws, antennae, and the symmetry of the legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with the claws and legs visible from a different angle.
description_view_3: Dorsal (top-down) view showing the segmentation of the back, the arrangement of the legs, and the placement of the antennae.
